  "findings": [
    "Loud, rhythmic metallic tapping/clacking noise at idle",
    "Frequency of noise is half of engine RPM, indicating a valvetrain or camshaft-speed timing issue",
    "Severe slack in timing chain or excessive valve clearance (collapsed lifter/bucket)"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Shut down engine immediately to prevent catastrophic valve-to-piston contact",
    "Remove valve cover to inspect timing chain tensioner, guides, and camshaft lobes",
    "Measure valve clearances and check for collapsed hydraulic lifters"
  ],
  "rationale": "The audio recording reveals a distinct, heavy metallic tapping sound at a frequency consistent with camshaft speed (approximately 8-10 Hz, which corresponds to a 4-cylinder engine idling around 1000-1200 RPM with a single-cylinder or single-valve-event defect, or a loose timing chain slapping the guide). This indicates a critical mechanical fault in the timing or valvetrain assembly that risks catastrophic engine failure if left unaddressed.",
  "references": [
    "OEM Service Manual - Engine Mechanical Diagnostics (Valvetrain Noise)",
    "OEM Service Manual - Timing Chain Wear and Tensioner Inspection Procedures"
  ]
